order: A trusted Agent task envelope publishes the exact result grammar before autonomous execution; invalid returned identifiers stop at validation and do not become an approval prompt, sanitized result, or automatic retry.

A replacement Production candidate returns only to shadow, repeats the complete signed activation chain, and blocks runtime use until the newest candidate is enforced without deleting the prior lineage.
A-F is procedural only; the applicable AGENTS.MD remains the invariant authority and exact local absence is the sole parent-fallback trigger.

The A-F overlay remains the compatibility procedure until the additive Next Workflow is activated through its externally trusted launcher; the paused Control Center is presentation only and is not a Production prerequisite.

The additive Next Workflow selects L1-L5 rigor independently of these compatibility rows; L1-L4 stay within the original task authority, while L5 has one explicit post-plan implementation approval boundary.

Protected non-UI CLI execution becomes Production-authoritative only through the externally installed launcher, immutable Git candidate, signed source/release/transition evidence, exact owner authority, real containment, persisted recovery proof, and review-gated result closure; live provider discovery retains a read-only host root and binds its pinned executable below a private writable tmpfs, while unavailable isolation stops with guidance.

Private non-executed complete CLI/native provider descriptors may make bootstrap, installed-launcher integrity, and injected provider-discovery or development-selection regressions portable on CI without Codex, but they grant no discovery, certification, provider execution, release, or Activation authority; containment executables are never simulated, and their absence requires explicit positive bootstrap and launcher-integrity skips plus passing guided refusal.

The immutable Owner Controller installer resolves the per-worktree checkout instance through the checkout-identity owner after clean synchronized-main validation; every Owner action resolves the same persisted identity read-only, the wrapper pins the verified Controller base, and tracked repository configuration never supplies local checkout authority.

A successful provider exit may wait only through the bounded grace period while protected process identity remains unknown; matched or reused identity returns immediately, and unresolved identity fails closed.

Runtime reconciliation is a confirmation-bound immutable Owner Controller action; the Production launcher continues stripping Owner authority and is not an additional conjunctive requirement for recovery.

Owner recovery uses structured L5 security-control planning; a terminal Agent launch settles only after protected process absence and persisted launch verification, without result acceptance or retry authority.
